text: "I cannot make you another Dispel Potion unless you can convince the Dryad to give you another magic acorn.  I do not think that is likely.
speaker: healer

text: Please leave me to make the potion and when you return I shall have it for you.
speaker: healer

text: "If you know the ingredients for a Dispel Potion and bring them here, I can make you such a potion."
speaker: healer

text: Let's see, to make the Dispel Potion I still need: %s %s %s %s %s
speaker: healer

text: You pay the Healer for the potion and put it away.
speaker: narrator

text: "I hope this will help you."
speaker: healer

text: You find that you have less money than you thought.  You can't afford to buy the potion.
speaker: narrator

text: "Oh, by the way, if you happen to find a ring on your adventures, I lost my favorite gold ring.  I will give a reward to the one who returns it."
speaker: healer

text: You can buy potions, get information, or sell ingredients here.
speaker: narrator

text: You see a clean, but very cluttered little hut filled with bottles and potions.  The Healer is busy puttering around with her potions.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a small flying creature preening itself .  A ladder leads to the loft above.  A pot over the fire simmers with herbs.  It is pleasantly warm in here.
speaker: narrator

text: You see a middle-aged woman with rosy cheeks and smile lines around her eyes.
speaker: narrator

text: You see a winged lizard much like the one you saw outside.
speaker: narrator

text: You see bottles and flasks of a wide variety of different potions.
speaker: narrator

text: Whatever is boiling there is either soup or some sort of potion.
speaker: narrator

text: Soup is good for you.  Mmmm Mmmm good.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like a ladder.
speaker: narrator

text: It's not your soup.
speaker: narrator

text: "Come back in the morning, please."
speaker: healer

text: Don't be nosy.
speaker: narrator

text: You can look but don't touch.
speaker: narrator

text: A rather motherly-looking woman with a look of vagueness that belies her shrewdness.  She wears an apron over her dress and a scarf wrapped around her head.
speaker: narrator

text: "Those fairies are cute, but they play rough!"
speaker: healer

text: "It is shaped in gold like a braid of the herb Althelas with entwined leaves."
speaker: healer

text: "I don't know how I lost it.  I hardly ever take it off."
speaker: healer

text: "You already have your reward."
speaker: healer

text: "I will give six golds to the person who returns my ring.  It was a gift when I graduated from the college of Healing."
speaker: healer

text: "I will pay you for Cheetaur claws, Troll beard, magic mushrooms, and flowers from Erana's Peace."
speaker: healer

text: "I use them to make my potions."
speaker: healer

text: "This potion is used to heal damage and keep wounds from getting worse.  It has gotten pretty dangerous around here, and a Healing Potion can be quite useful in a serious situation."
speaker: healer

text: "Vigor Potion is used to revitalize yourself after vigorous exercise.   It helps to restore stamina."
speaker: healer

text: "Magic Potions restore the energy needed to cast spells."
speaker: healer

text: "I make up potions that heal injuries.  I will be happy to sell you a Healing Potion if you like."
speaker: healer

text: "I make and sell Healing Potions, Magic Potions, Vigor Potions and Undead Unguent."
speaker: healer

text:
"The Potions will cost you: 
            Healing Potion 40 silvers 
            Magic Potion 60 silvers 
            Vigor Potion 20 silvers 
            Undead Unguent 100 silvers."
speaker: healer

text: "Undead Unguent is used to drive off the minor undead such as zombies or floating spirits.  It doesn't last long, so you should use it only when you are anticipating an encounter with such things."
speaker: healer

text: "There are not too many zombies in this land... too damp, I suppose.  There are some floating spirits, I have heard."
speaker: healer

text: "Floating Spirits are the ghosts which usually hang around graveyards at night.  They are dangerous, so don't go near them unless you use Undead Unguent first."
speaker: healer

text: "Stamina is a measure of the energy the body uses as you work or play."
speaker: healer

text: "I've never heard of a monster around here with green fur.  Rare things are sometimes by their nature magical."
speaker: healer

text: "Fairy Dust obviously comes from fairies.  They dance around mushroom rings when it is night.  Fairies are magical beings, so be careful around them."
speaker: healer

text: "I use flowers from Erana's Peace to the north in nearly all my potions.  They have some of the magic of the place even when they are dried."
speaker: healer

text: "I will pay you five silvers for a flask full of flowers."
speaker: healer

text: "I use the mushrooms from a fairy ring to make Magic Potions."
speaker: healer

text: "I will pay you one gold for some."
speaker: healer

text: "The Cheetaur looks like a cross between a panther and a man.  It is vicious and tough.  Unless you are a very tough fighter, you had best try to get away from it.  If you do manage to kill it, then I will pay you five silvers for each claw."
speaker: healer

text: "The Trolls around here are tough monsters that cannot stand the light of day and so are found at night or in caves.  They are very difficult to kill, but I will pay two healing potions for the beard of a Troll."
speaker: healer

text: "I have heard that the Dryad of the woods knows a Dispel Potion to disenchant people with spells upon them."
speaker: healer

text: "I'm not sure what kind of water that is."
speaker: healer

text: "The only place you can get a magic acorn from is a Dryad's oak tree."
speaker: healer

text: "I will pay you one silver for each empty flask you bring me so I can use it for more potions.  Waste not, want not, I always say."
speaker: healer

text: "Well, my name is Amelia Appleberry, but mostly I am just known as the Healer around here."
speaker: healer

text: "Oh, that's my pet, Pterry, the pterosaur.  He has a girl friend, Pteresa, who has a nest in the oak outside my door.  Pterry keeps me company and listens to my chatter as I work."
speaker: healer

text: "They are a species of flying lizard.  I understand they can grow quite large in the south."
speaker: healer

text: "Poor man, he hasn't been the same since his son and daughter were taken from him."
speaker: healer

text: "He was just a young dashing man when he last rode off and never returned."
speaker: healer

text: "The dear child, I can still picture her with her beautiful blond hair done up in braids.  She was so sweet.  It's hard to believe she's gone."
speaker: healer

text: "Those brutes!  I'm always having to heal someone they've beaten up and robbed.  I hate thieves and brigands."
speaker: healer

text: "Would you believe the brigands almost killed him a while back?  Fortunately their leader made them bring Heinrich here where I could heal him."
speaker: healer

text: "Almost due north of here is the meadow called Erana's Peace.  It is a very magical area and it is a place of safety.  It is beautiful all year, for the flowers are always in bloom."
speaker: healer

text: "I have the skill to use my magic to create potions.  I am a bit proud about it.  Not everyone can do that, you know."
speaker: healer

text: "Mandrake root is used in a variety of spells, mostly for evil purposes.  Mandrake must be pulled from a deadman's grave at midnight.  The root is particularly powerful."
speaker: healer

text: "I'm sorry, I just don't know much about that.  Perhaps you should ask someone else."
speaker: healer

text: "Oh, thank you for finding my ring.  How I've missed this.  Here are six golds and two Healing Potions for your reward!"
speaker: healer

text: Thank you, but I have all the flowers I need.
speaker: healer

text:
"Thank you.  I often use flowers from Erana's Peace in potion making.  Here are your silvers."
You put them away.

say: Thank you. I often use flowers from Erana's Peace in potion making. Here are your silvers.

text: Thank you, but I have enough mushrooms.

text:
"These are very nice.  I'll dry them and grind them into a powder.  Let me think...oh yes! I said these are worth a gold.  Here you are."
You put it away.

say: These are very nice. I'll dry them and grind them into a powder. Let me think...oh yes! I said these are worth a gold. Here you are.

text:
"Do you mean to tell me that you actually managed to kill a Cheetaur?  You're quite a hero, aren't you?  Here is your money.
You might think about purchasing a Healing Potion in case you have to fight such monsters again."
speaker: healer

text:
"Don't tell me you actually killed a Troll?  Why, you are really amazing.  I haven't had any Troll's Beard for a long time.  Here are two Healing Potions for the beard."
You put them away.

say: Don't tell me you actually killed a Troll? Why, you are really amazing. I haven't had any Troll's Beard for a long time. Here are two Healing Potions for the beard.

text: "So, you say this is one of the ingredients for the Dispel Potion.  Well, I had better get started making it."

text: "Well, what can I do for you this time?"

text: "Lovely day, isn't it?  My, you look very healthy for an adventurer.  You must be new."
speaker: healer

text: "What can I do for you?  I can sell you potions if you like.  I also buy spell components if you are interested in gathering some for me."
speaker: healer

text: "Don't mind me, I always have so much to do around here."
speaker: healer

text: You say to the Healer "I have been to visit the Dryad of the Woods.  She gave me a Magic Acorn and told me the formula for a potion to dispel enchantments!"

text: "Here is the Potion of Dispel!  Use it by splashing it on the victim of a magic spell.  Mind you, this won't work if the spell is caused by a magic item rather than a cast spell."
speaker: healer

text: "Thank you so much.  Oh, I'm so happy to have my ring back!"
speaker: healer

text: "The Dispel Potion will take a while to make.  Why don't you come back later?"
speaker: healer

text: "You won't be needing your old leather armor anymore.  I shall give you a 50 silver trade-in discount."

text: "Here you are; thank you for your patronage."

text: This looks like a dry goods store, but it smells like a musty library.  The stove feels nice on such a crisp day.  Behind the counter and on the shelves, there are many and various items for sale.  The shopkeeper appears to ignore you while he reads a book.
speaker: narrator

text: The stove is burning merrily away, making the shop seem almost too warm.
speaker: narrator

text: The shopkeeper is a small, balding man who wears glasses.  He looks as if he can barely lift the book he is reading.  Judging from his clothes, he is fairly well-to-do.
speaker: narrator

text: The barrels appear to be filled with something and look rather heavy.
speaker: narrator

text: The jars of beets and sauerkraut look like they were canned locally.
speaker: narrator

text: Behind the counter are weapons, armor, and such equipment as you would more often find in an "Adventurer's Shop".
speaker: narrator

text: You're not close enough to the counter to see behind it.
speaker: narrator

text: On the counter and on the shelves are assortments of canned goods, honey jars, sewing items, and odds and ends that people in a small town would need.
speaker: narrator

text: The leather armor is more like barding, designed to be used under clothing.  The chain mail is much heavier, and looks very strong.
speaker: narrator

text: There are barrels of flour and salt next to the counter.  There seem to be jars of pickled pigs' feet, linen cloth, and other things in which you have very little interest.
speaker: narrator

text: There are a variety of items of the sort an adventurer might need.
speaker: narrator

text: The shopkeeper doesn't seem to hear you.
speaker: narrator

text: Either the shopkeeper can't hear you or he's ignoring you.  Try striding right up to the counter.
speaker: narrator

text: Go ahead. Talk to him.
speaker: narrator

text: "Unfortunately, I have only standard weapons and equipment, " the shopkeeper tells you.  "I carry daggers and chain armor.  Maybe someday I'll be able to carry magic ones, though."
speaker: shopkeeper
say: Unfortunately, I have only standard weapons and equipment. I carry daggers and chain armor. Maybe someday I'll be able to carry magic ones, though.

text: "I can also sell you food or empty flasks for carrying liquids or powders."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "You can get really good protection from my chain mail armor.  It's very heavy though, and I would have to charge you 500 silvers for it."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "Ah!  The use of the dagger is a most skillful art.  Actually, this particular weapon is longer than most, but still easily concealable.  A bargain at 20 silvers."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "Adventuring rations aren't the tastiest food in the world, but they will keep you healthy and alert as you go along.  A pack of 5 rations will cost you just 5 silvers."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "Oh, no, I don't carry that sort of thing here.  If you want to get drunk, try the tavern."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "It's a very good idea to carry an empty flask or two, in case you want to pick up a liquid or something else that needs a container.  Our flasks are a great bargain at 2 silvers each."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "Oh, this book?  It's about an adventurer who is trying to become a Hero.  The title is 'Quest for Glory:  A Hero's Death'."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "Oh, you don't want those.  They're for the people who live here in town.  Adventurers just need adventuring equipment."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "The Sheriff and Otto protect this town.  The Sheriff used to be a real adventurer, you know!"
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "Why, that's me!  Kaspar!"
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "Hilde sells fine produce for a reasonable price."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "I don't like to talk about other people behind their backs."

text: "Well, my adventurer friend!  Would you like to make a purchase this time?"

text: "My, you look like an adventurer!  We don't see too many around here.  The brigands tend to get rid of most adventurers before they make it to town."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "You may be in luck.  I carry a modest amount of adventurer's equipment behind the counter.  Would you like to make a purchase?"
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "Oh, good! I do try to stock some things adventurers can use.  I actually would rather be an adventurer than a shopkeeper, you see."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "My items are pretty ordinary, though.  You probably already have most of them if you've done any adventuring at all."

text: "Now what was that you wanted again?"

text: Well, look around anyway.  You may find something you could use."

text: You see blue Meeps, purple Meeps, and occasionally a green Meep.  They seem to be very shy; whenever you approach one, it hides under its rock.
speaker: narrator

text: Large, heavy-looking rocks cover the many holes.  The Meeps must be stronger than they look to lift these rocks so easily.

text: You keep seeing differently-colored Meeps come out of each hole.  They either lead to large underground caverns, or they are all connected under the earth.

text: Do you know the difference between a Cheetaur and a comma?

text: Spoilsport!
speaker: erasmus

text: A Cheetaur has claws at the end of its paws, and a comma is a pause at the end of a clause!

text:
Do you know what's big, purple, and eats ungefaehr-
liche dinge?
speaker: erasmus

text: Well...let me figure out something tougher.
speaker: erasmus

text:
Big, purple ungefaehr-
liche dinge eaters!
speaker: erasmus

text:
Large purple ungefaehr-
liche dinge eaters are all too common in the forest these days.
speaker: erasmus

text: Do you know which has more legs, one cow or no cow?
speaker: erasmus

text: Too easy, huh?  Well, I'll just have to think of a better one.
speaker: erasmus

text: You're right!  NO cow has more than four legs.  These are too easy.
speaker: erasmus

text: Do you know the difference between a wounded Triceratops and a magic user casting the "Fly" spell?
speaker: erasmus

text: Darn.  I thought I made that one up myself!
speaker: erasmus

text: One is a raging Saurus and the other is a soaring Magus!
speaker: erasmus

text: I figured one was a dying lizard and the other was a flying wizard.
speaker: fenrus

text: Sorry, Fenrus, but "He who tells the jokes gets to make up the punch lines".
speaker: erasmus

text: Did you hear the joke about the witches broom?
speaker: erasmus

text: Oh.  That's a pity!
speaker: erasmus

text: That's odd.  It's sweeping the valley!
speaker: erasmus

text: That one usually floors me, but I'm going to brush it aside before I'm swept away with laughter.

text: You don't have any.
speaker: narrator

text: The mushrooms from the Fairy Ring are pretty good, so you hear.  However, when they're mixed with Kobold toadstools...that's a horse of a different color.
speaker: narrator

text: You eat some of the Kobold's fungus.  Within seconds your insides feel like they're on fire.  Guess you're not a Kobold, huh?
speaker: narrator

text: You think that you'd better take it easy for a while until you recover.
speaker: narrator

text: You ingest a few of the mushrooms from the Faery Ring.  They taste delicious.
speaker: narrator

text: Wow!  Look at all the nice paisley horses!
speaker: narrator

text: Not to mention the beautiful neon sky.
speaker: narrator

text: That's funny.  I could have sworn I warned you about eating too many Magic Mushrooms.  Your mind permanently warped, you die a garishly polka-dotted death.
speaker: narrator

text: Hmm, that was interesting.  But it would probably not be a good idea to repeat the experience.
speaker: narrator

text: You eat the Magic Acorn.  It tastes terrible.  Better stick to real food next time.
speaker: narrator

text: There's no need.  You're not hungry.
speaker: narrator

text: You eat some of the apples.  They actually taste quite good.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't have any fruit.
speaker: narrator

text:
You eat some vegetables.  You think they would have been better saved for cows or horses.
Real Heroes eat preserved dry rations.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't have any vegetables.
speaker: narrator

text: The rations are tasteless but filling.
speaker: narrator

text: You aren't carrying any rations.
speaker: narrator

text: Ugh.  You don't want to eat *that*.
speaker: narrator

why: credits screen text, not spoken prose

text:
DURING THE GAME:
Click at the top of the screen or press ESC to use the menus. Additional shortcuts are shown there.
Click Right or Shift-Click Left Mouse
Button on an object to look at it.

     IN TYPING WINDOWS:
Arrows, Home and End move the cursor, or click anywhere with the mouse. Ctrl-C clears the line.

     IN DIALOG WINDOWS:
Select the outlined item with <Enter> and
use Tab and Shift-Tab to move between 
choices. 
Or click with the mouse to select an item.

ESC always cancels.
speaker: narrator

text:
You can change your mind
   if you have one.
speaker: narrator

text: Every hero needs a break now and then.  Have a nice nap.
speaker: narrator

text: Sorry, there's not enough room to see that here.
speaker: narrator

text: "Even as we fought, I could detect your skills improving through practice."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "It was most prudent that you should give in to my superior skill."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Still, you seem to tire more easily than you should."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Be more aware of the angle with which your weapon approaches your opponent.  The thrust bites quick and deep, but the swing will do more damage if it connects."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "The art of dancing would be a good skill for you to practice, my friend.  The balance of the body over the rapidly moving feet has won many a contest."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "You have a tendency to overbalance when you dodge a blow.  This will adversely affect the timing and effectiveness of your counterattack."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Put your attention to the control of your legs when you duck a swing.  Your springiness makes you seem to bounce, and a good opponent (like myself) will always take advantage of the opening."
speaker: weapon-master

text:
"If you would like a stretcher, my weak, tired friend...
You seem a shadow of the person who began this little lesson."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Should we meet again, I would not be adverse to another go-round.  Farewell, friend!"
speaker: weapon-master

text: You are in the courtyard of Castle Spielburg.
speaker: narrator

text: The courtyard is paved with granite paving stones, carefully placed by a skilled craftsman.
speaker: narrator

text: There is nobody else here.
speaker: narrator

text: There are two guards near the castle door.
speaker: narrator

text: He isn't here at the moment.
speaker: narrator

text: What you see, along with what you smell, gives the impression that the building on the east side of the courtyard contains the stables.
speaker: narrator

text: On the west side of the courtyard is a building that looks like soldier's barracks.
speaker: narrator

text: As a matter of fact, there's a soldier sleeping in front of it now.
speaker: narrator

text: The door to the Baron's castle is well guarded.
speaker: narrator

text: As you look back at the castle gate, you see a line of bushes along the south wall and the gatehouse and portcullis.
speaker: narrator

text: You barely get to sleep when you hear the guards searching the castle grounds for bums.  You decide it's time to make like a tree and leave.
speaker: narrator

text: There are too many guards around.
speaker: narrator

text: This tastes as sour as it smells, and it burns your throat as you swallow it. Still, it isn't the worst beer you've ever drunk.
speaker: narrator

text: You know, that actually tasted fine! This really isn't such a bad place, after all, and the bartender reminds you of an old friend you used to know.
speaker: narrator

text: Suddenly, you don't feel so good...
speaker: narrator

text: Smells like Troll's Sweat. Tastes like Troll's Sweat. By golly, it IS Troll's Sweat....
speaker: narrator

text: You've never tasted anything like it before.
speaker: narrator

text: Too much beer.
speaker: narrator

text: ...and one Troll's Sweat is too many.
speaker: narrator

text: Ohhhhhhhhh woooowwwwwww!!!
speaker: hero

text: Maybe you really shouldn't have tried the Dragon's Breath!  Better luck next time, and we hope you saved your game.
speaker: narrator

text: The late frosts of winter give way to to the greenery of spring. There is the crisp smell of cedar in the air. This forest seems to be unusually still and quiet.
speaker: narrator

text: It IS a ghost...a transparent, decaying, writhing, slimy, undead fragment of undulating ectoplasm!
speaker: narrator

text: You might be near a graveyard!
speaker: narrator

text: There are no ghosts here to look at.
speaker: narrator

text: Pine needles, moss and early spring grasses cover the forest floor.
speaker: narrator

text: The low-lying bushes form tight tangles between the trees.
speaker: narrator

text: The valley floor is covered by fine, moist soil with few large rocks.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't get ghosts.  Ghosts get YOU!
speaker: narrator

text: There aren't any.
speaker: narrator

text: That isn't drinkable -- it's an oil.
speaker: narrator

text: You pry at the sealed lid of the chest, and...
speaker: narrator

text: You hack and pry at the chest, but you are not strong enough to force its lid.
speaker: narrator

text: You hear a very satisfying "Snick" as your pick catches the lock just right.  Then you sense powerful magics dissipating around the lock, and your hands shake as you realize you have narrowly avoided some sort of Magical Doom.
speaker: narrator

text: Your fingers shift slightly, and you feel an odd prickling sensation coming from the lock.  You have a bad feeling about this.
speaker: narrator

text: Mighty careless of you to walk right in front of that knife!  Or did the Chief Thief hit you on purpose?  Or did you just discover an obscure bug in the program???  Maybe you'll never really know.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't believe the Chief Thief's reflexes as he instantly reacts to your intention to pull a fast one.  A dagger flies like lightning and....
speaker: narrator

text: Trouble with sharp, pointy objects

text: You cannot escape this encounter.
speaker: narrator

speaker: narrator

text: This might not be the best time for practicing non-combat spells.
speaker: narrator

text: Go ahead!
speaker: narrator

text: You're too close.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't have time for that.
speaker: narrator

text: Caught in combat with neither sword nor dagger, you are unable to resist the monster's fierce onslaught.  Try to be better equipped in your next life.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the brigand leader.  From the look in her eyes you've only got seconds to live.
speaker: narrator

text: Casting a spell isn't useful now.
speaker: narrator

text: She's not in a talking mood.
speaker: narrator

text: Not a good idea.
speaker: narrator

text: No time for that.
speaker: narrator

text: The Brigand leader looks tough!  This may be the toughest battle of your career so far.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a small sign which reads "Booby Trap".  You are now in a pitiful situation as you hear the brigands approach to see the booby they've caught.  You think to yourself, "If it looks like a trap, and it feels like a trap, then maybe it IS a trap".
speaker: hero
